title: Embedded Metric Format (EMF) stops emitting metrics after upgrading to syn-nodejs-puppeteer-11.0 or later
symptoms: Embedded Metric Format metrics stop being emitted after upgrading to syn-nodejs-puppeteer-11.0 or later
CloudWatch does not parse EMF JSON from canary logs
Log messages contain a canaryRunId= prefix before the JSON payload
Setting CW_SYNTHETICS_LOG_FORMAT=TEXT does not resolve the issue
Same canary script works correctly on syn-nodejs-puppeteer-10.0
root_cause:
Starting with syn-nodejs-puppeteer-11.0, the Synthetics runtime automatically inserts a canaryRunId= prefix into all log messages. This prefix is required for loading canary logs in the Synthetics console and cannot be disabled.

The CloudWatch Embedded Metric Format (EMF) specification requires that the LogEvent message be a valid JSON object with no additional data at the beginning or end. The canaryRunId= prefix breaks this requirement, causing the EMF parser to fail silently — no metrics are extracted from the log event.

This affects any canary that emits custom metrics via EMF by logging JSON payloads with the _aws CloudWatchMetrics structure. The canaryRunId prefix is separate from structured logging and persists even when CW_SYNTHETICS_LOG_FORMAT is set to TEXT.

recommendations.title: Use direct CloudWatch Logs PutLogEvents to bypass the canaryRunId prefix
recommendations.problem: Synthetics runtime prepends canaryRunId= to all log messages, breaking EMF format

solution.step: Create a CloudWatch Logs client and emit EMF logs directly
solution.description: Bypass the Synthetics logging helper by calling PutLogEvents directly. This writes pure JSON to the log stream without the canaryRunId prefix.
solution.expected_outcome: EMF metrics are emitted successfully. Metrics appear in CloudWatch Metrics under the specified namespace.

solution.description: Confirm that metrics are being extracted from the direct log events
solution.command:
# Via AWS Console:
# 1. Navigate to CloudWatch > Metrics > All metrics
# 2. Search for your custom namespace
# 3. Verify metric data points are appearing
#
# Note: Logs emitted via PutLogEvents will NOT appear in the
# Synthetics console. View them directly in the log stream
# or via CloudWatch Logs Insights.
solution.expected_outcome: Custom metrics appear in CloudWatch Metrics. Log events are visible in CloudWatch Logs Insights.

recommendations.title: Downgrade to syn-nodejs-puppeteer-10.0 as a temporary workaround
recommendations.problem: Need immediate fix while refactoring metric emission code

solution.description: Version 10.0 does not insert the canaryRunId prefix, so EMF works as expected
solution.expected_outcome: EMF metrics resume emitting correctly. Note: version 10.0 lacks features and fixes from newer runtimes.
